2606.07365.title: A robust PPG foundation model using multimodal physiological supervision
2606.07365.paper_summary: Photoplethysmography (PPG), a non-invasive measure of changes in blood volume, is widely used in both wearable devices and clinical settings. Recent PPG foundation models either use open-source ICU datasets with pretraining paradigms that require curated data and thus complicate generalization to field-like data, or use closed-source field-like PPG data. In contrast, we propose a PPG foundation model that does not require high-quality or field-like pretraining data, and instead leverages accompanying electrocardiogram and respiratory signals in ICU datasets to select contrastive samples during pretraining. Our approach allows the model to retain and learn from noisy PPG segments, improving robustness at inference. Our model, pretrained on 3x fewer subjects than existing state-of-the-art approaches, achieves performance improvements on 14 out of 15 diverse downstream tasks, including field-like daily activity and heart rate prediction. Our results demonstrate that multimodal supervision can integrate complementary physiological information to improve the robustness of PPG foundation models and enhance their generalization to consumer-grade data.
2606.07365.paper_summary_zh: 光電容積描記法（PPG）是一種非侵入性測量血容量變化的方法，廣泛應用於可穿戴設備和臨床環境中。最近的PPG基礎模型要麼使用開源的ICU數據集，並且預訓練範式需要經過整理的數據，從而使得對類似現場數據的泛化變得複雜；要麼使用封閉源的類似現場的PPG數據。相較之下，我們提出了一種不需要高品質或類似現場預訓練數據的PPG基礎模型，而是利用ICU數據集中伴隨的心電圖和呼吸信號，在預訓練期間選擇對比樣本。我們的方法使模型能夠保留並從噪聲PPG片段中學習，提高推理時的穩健性。我們的模型在比現有最先進的方法少3倍的受試者上進行預訓練，在15個不同的下游任務中有14個任務的性能得到了改善，包括類似現場的日常活動和心率預測。我們的結果顯示，多模態監督可以整合互補的生理信息，以提高PPG基礎模型的穩健性並增強其對消費級數據的泛化能力。
2606.07365.author: Eloy Geenjaar et.al.
2606.07365.authors: Eloy Geenjaar, Vince Calhoun, Scott Daly, Gouthaman KV, Lie Lu, Trisha Mittal, Daniel P. Darcy
